status_legend.in_progress: The feature is the current active task.
status_legend.blocked: Work cannot continue until a documented blocker is resolved.
status_legend.passing: Required verification has passed and evidence is recorded.

features.name: Document ingest & chunking
features.description: Parse uploaded PDF/PPTX/DOCX files and split into chunks by heading/slide boundary (not a hard character count), producing chunks[] with doc_id, page, and chunk_id. See docs/concept-graph-pipeline.md step 1.
features.user_visible_behavior: POST /ingest on packages/gpu-worker accepts multipart file uploads (PDF/PPTX/DOCX) and returns {"chunks": [...]} with doc_id/chunk_id/page/text populated per chunk. /embed is left for feat-002 (embedding + concept extraction).
verification: Run packages/gpu-worker with a sample PDF/PPTX/DOCX from data/ and confirm chunks[] is returned with doc_id, page, chunk_id populated for each chunk.
Confirm no chunk splits a heading/slide boundary in the middle for the sample set.
evidence: packages/gpu-worker/worker/ingest.py implements chunk_document() (PDF: one chunk per page via PyMuPDF; PPTX: one chunk per slide via python-pptx; DOCX: one chunk per Heading-delimited section via python-docx).

packages/gpu-worker/tests/test_ingest.py — 4/4 passed (`python -m pytest tests/`): PDF splits by page, PPTX splits by slide, DOCX splits on heading boundaries without cutting a section in half, unsupported extensions raise ValueError.

Manually exercised POST /ingest via FastAPI TestClient with a synthetic PDF: 200 response, {'chunks': [{'doc_id': 'sample.pdf', 'chunk_id': 'sample.pdf:p1', 'page': 1, 'text': 'Test ingest endpoint page one.'}]}.

No real sample PDF/PPTX/DOCX exists in data/ yet (only the mastery-tree sample JSON) — verified against synthetic fixtures built in the test file instead; re-verify against the actual demo dataset once feat-009 picks a document set.
features.notes: worker/main.py's /embed now implements feat-002. docx has no rendered page numbers, so its `page` field is the 1-indexed heading-delimited section instead — documented in ingest.py.

features.name: Embedding + concept extraction & clustering
features.description: Vectorize chunks with sentence-transformers (bge-large/e5-large) on ROCm-enabled PyTorch, extract raw_concepts[] per chunk via Gemma (2B/7B) served locally through vLLM-ROCm/Ollama, then cluster/dedupe into canonical_concepts[] via cosine similarity + Louvain clustering (no LLM call needed for dedupe). See docs/concept-graph-pipeline.md steps 2-3.
features.user_visible_behavior: packages/gpu-worker/worker/main.py's /embed endpoint returns canonical_concepts[] (name, short definition, originating chunk_id) instead of the current stub.
verification: Run the sample demo dataset through the endpoint and confirm canonical_concepts[] merges near-duplicate phrasings (e.g. 'gradient descent' vs 'steepest-descent optimization') into one node.
Confirm Gemma is invoked via a local ROCm/Ollama endpoint, not a hosted API call — this is required for the Best Use of Gemma bonus and AMD platform usage criterion (see docs/brd.md §4.3, §7.3).
evidence: packages/gpu-worker/worker/concepts.py implements GemmaConceptExtractor (HTTP client against {GEMMA_BASE_URL}/api/generate — an Ollama-style local endpoint, default http://localhost:11434), SentenceTransformerEmbedder (lazy-loaded bge-large-en-v1.5), and cluster_concepts (cosine similarity + networkx.algorithms.community.louvain_communities, no LLM call).

packages/gpu-worker/tests/test_concepts.py — 4/4 passed: confirms GemmaConceptExtractor POSTs to '{base_url}/api/generate' (asserted literally, so a regression to a hosted-API URL would fail this test); confirms cluster_concepts merges 'gradient descent' and 'the steepest-descent optimization algorithm' into one canonical concept (shorter name wins) while keeping 'linear regression' separate; confirms extract_raw_concepts aggregates across chunks; confirms empty input returns [].

packages/gpu-worker/tests/test_main_embed.py — 1/1 passed: exercises the real POST /embed endpoint end-to-end via FastAPI TestClient with fakes swapped in through app.dependency_overrides (get_gemma_extractor, get_embedder), proving the endpoint wiring itself (not just the underlying functions) works.
Full suite: `python -m pytest tests/` — 9/9 passed (4 feat-001 + 5 feat-002, no regressions). `python -m compileall worker tests` — pass.

Not run against a live local Gemma/Ollama server or a real sentence-transformers model download — both are behind fakes in the test suite by design (network/GPU-independent tests). Re-verify against a real vLLM-ROCm/Ollama instance on the AMD Developer Cloud box once that's provisioned (see docs/hackathon-scope.md §5 Day-1 ROCm check).
features.notes: GEMMA_BASE_URL env var controls the local endpoint (default http://localhost:11434, matching Ollama's default port); no hosted-API fallback is wired in on purpose. Update: CanonicalConcept now also carries `id` (concept_NNN) and `embedding` (cluster centroid), returned from /embed — added while implementing feat-003, which needs both for pair pre-filtering without re-embedding concepts from scratch.

features.name: Dependency-graph construction (prerequisite inference)
features.description: For pre-filtered concept pairs only (high embedding similarity or nearby co-occurrence), call Fireworks AI (Llama 3.x) to infer directed prerequisite edges with a confidence score. See docs/concept-graph-pipeline.md step 4 and the O(n^2) cost-avoidance note.
features.user_visible_behavior: packages/gpu-worker/worker/main.py's POST /build-graph endpoint takes /embed's concepts[] output and returns {"edges": [...]} (candidate_edges[] as from/to/confidence, keyed by concept id) instead of the current NotImplementedError stub.
verification: Confirm the number of Fireworks calls scales with pre-filtered pairs, not all-pairs O(n^2), for the demo dataset.
Spot-check a few candidate_edges[] against the sample tree in docs/concept-graph-pipeline.md for plausibility.
evidence: packages/gpu-worker/worker/prerequisites.py implements pre_filter_pairs (cosine similarity over concept embeddings from feat-002's /embed output) and FireworksClient (hosted OpenAI-compatible chat completions — a hosted call is fine here, unlike Gemma extraction, since this isn't the AMD-usage/Gemma-bonus story) and build_candidate_edges.

packages/gpu-worker/tests/test_prerequisites.py — 4/4 passed. test_pre_filter_pairs_avoids_full_all_pairs_scan directly proves the O(n^2) avoidance: 6 concepts in 3 tight similarity clusters yield C(6,2)=15 possible pairs, but pre_filter_pairs returns exactly the 3 within-cluster pairs. test_build_candidate_edges_only_calls_fireworks_for_prefiltered_pairs asserts the fake Fireworks client is called exactly 3 times (once per pre-filtered pair), not 15.

packages/gpu-worker/tests/test_main_build_graph.py — 1/1 passed: exercises the real POST /build-graph endpoint via FastAPI TestClient + app.dependency_overrides (no real Fireworks API key/network needed).
Full suite: `python -m pytest tests/` — 14/14 passed (4 feat-001 + 5 feat-002 + 5 feat-003, no regressions). `python -m compileall worker tests` — pass.

Not yet spot-checked against the actual sample tree in docs/concept-graph-pipeline.md — that requires a real Fireworks API key and a real concept set from feat-002 run end-to-end; do this once feat-009 picks the demo dataset.
Not run against a live Fireworks API — FireworksClient is only verified against a monkeypatched httpx.post.
features.notes: Corrected this feature's `area` from "api" to "gpu-worker" — docs/architecture.md's Data contract section says gpu-worker produces the candidate graph (including inferred prerequisite edges) and hands it to api, which starts at the self-checking validation loop (feat-004). The original area value was inconsistent with that. CanonicalConcept (feat-002) gained `id` and `embedding` fields to support this feature's pre-filtering — see concepts.py.

features.name: Self-checking graph agent (cycle/redundancy repair)
features.description: Build a networkx.DiGraph from candidate_edges[], run nx.simple_cycles(), and repair any cycle found by dropping its lowest-confidence edge (bounded retry loop; force-drop the weakest edge if a cycle survives N iterations). See docs/concept-graph-pipeline.md step 5.
features.user_visible_behavior: The API produces a valid_dag with zero cycles for the demo dataset, with a log of any edges the agent auto-dropped and why.
verification: Feed a deliberately cyclic edge set (e.g. an inverted prerequisite) through the repair loop and confirm it converges to a valid DAG within N iterations.
Confirm nx.simple_cycles() returns empty on the final graph.
features.notes: This is the 'agentic self-check' the hackathon differentiation story leans on (docs/hackathon-scope.md §3) — don't skip the repair-loop logging.

features.name: Topological sort → tiered learning path
features.description: Use nx.topological_generations() (not a plain topological_sort) on the valid_dag to assign each node a level, allowing parallel branches. See docs/concept-graph-pipeline.md step 6.
features.user_visible_behavior: Each node in the final graph JSON has a level field; apps/api/app/routers/trees.py's GET /trees/{topic_id} returns nodes[] + edges[] matching the schema in docs/concept-graph-pipeline.md instead of the current 501 stub.
verification: Confirm every edge's child level is strictly greater than its parent's level (DAG tier invariant) for the demo dataset.
Confirm GET /trees/{topic_id} returns real data, not the current HTTPException(501).

features.name: Skill-tree UI with unlock logic
features.description: Render nodes[]/edges[] with @xyflow/react, grouped into columns by level and colored by status (locked | unlocked | mastered). A node unlocks when every edge pointing into it is mastered.
features.user_visible_behavior: apps/web/app/tree/page.tsx shows an interactive skill tree fetched from data/atlas_mastery_tree_sample.json (dev) or GET {NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL}/trees/{topic_id}, replacing the current 'TODO: render mastery tree' placeholder.
verification: Load the sample tree JSON in the browser and confirm nodes render grouped by tier with correct lock/unlock coloring.
Manually mark a Tier-0 node mastered and confirm its children flip from locked to unlocked without a page reload.

features.name: Per-node lesson + quiz + real-world example (agentic RAG)
features.description: For a given node, retrieve only that node's source chunks (via chunk_id/doc_id carried through since step 1), then call Fireworks to generate a lesson, an MCQ checkpoint quiz, and a real-world example, with a self-check that the quiz matches the lesson before returning it.
features.user_visible_behavior: An endpoint returns { lesson, quiz, example, sources } for a node_id, scoped to that node's cited chunks only — not whole-corpus prompting.
verification: Confirm the retrieval set passed to Fireworks is limited to the node's own sources[], not the full corpus.
Confirm the self-check step rejects/regenerates a quiz that doesn't match its lesson at least once during testing (force a mismatch to verify the guard fires).
features.notes: Can proceed in parallel with feat-006 once feat-005 is done — both only need the tiered graph, not the finished UI.

features.name: Checkpoint quiz & unlock mechanic
features.description: Wire the MCQ quiz UI to POST /trees/{topic_id}/nodes/{node_id}/submit-quiz; on pass, mark the node mastered and unlock its children per feat-006's unlock logic.
features.user_visible_behavior: Passing a node's quiz in the UI immediately unlocks its downstream nodes, replacing the current 501 stub in apps/api/app/routers/trees.py's submit_quiz.
verification: Submit a passing quiz answer set and confirm the node's status flips to mastered and children flip to unlocked in the UI.
Submit a failing answer set and confirm the node stays locked/unlocked (not mastered) and children stay locked.

features.name: Pre-indexed, deterministic demo dataset
features.description: Run the full feat-001..feat-007 pipeline once, offline, on the chosen demo document set (10-30 docs, one domain) and save the static output so the live demo doesn't depend on Fireworks latency/availability during judging.
features.user_visible_behavior: data/atlas_mastery_tree_sample.json (or a topic-specific equivalent) is real pipeline output, not a hand-written placeholder, and the demo path loads it without calling Fireworks live.
verification: Run the demo end-to-end offline (no network calls to Fireworks) using only the pre-indexed JSON and confirm the full upload→graph→play loop still works.
Confirm the checked-in JSON matches the schema in docs/concept-graph-pipeline.md.
features.notes: This is the credit-runway and live-demo-reliability safeguard called out in docs/brd.md §II and docs/hackathon-scope.md §6 (Day 5).
